title: Astracantha rousseana
intro: Astracantha rousseana, also known as Russian astragalus, is a plant species within the legume family that has been used in traditional medicine for its therapeutic properties. The health benefits of Astracantha rousseana include its immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help to protect against infections and reduce inflammation in the body. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Astracantha rousseana is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions including respiratory issues and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Astracantha rousseana is a species of flowering plant in the legume family Fabaceae. It is native to the Mediterranean region and belongs to the subgenus Astracantha within the genus Astragalus. The subgenus Astracantha is a group of legumes that are often referred to as "milk-vetches" due to the presence of a milky sap when the stems are cut. Astracantha rousseana is a low-growing, spreading shrub with small, yellow or white flowers. This plant is an important part of the Mediterranean ecosystem, providing food and shelter for various insects and small animals.
